summary_explanations.health_score: A quick quality estimate based on detected risks. It is not a final grade. It tells you whether the project looks stable enough to keep building or needs cleanup first.
summary_explanations.file_count: How many files the analyzer found after ignoring folders like .git, node_modules, and virtual environments.
summary_explanations.functions_detected: How many Python functions were detected. More functions usually means the project has more behavior and more pieces to understand.
summary_explanations.routes_detected: How many Flask/FastAPI-style web routes were detected. A route usually means a page or API endpoint users can interact with.

technologies.name: Python
technologies.explanation: A general-purpose programming language. In your projects, it usually powers backend logic, automation, file scanning, or data processing.

technologies.name: GitHub/Versioning
technologies.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

technologies.name: SQLite
technologies.explanation: A lightweight database stored in a local file. Useful for personal apps because it does not require a separate database server.

technologies.name: HTML/CSS
technologies.explanation: The structure and styling of a web page. HTML creates the content; CSS controls how it looks.

technologies.name: API / Routes
technologies.explanation: A way for apps to request or send data. Routes are endpoints your app exposes; API calls are requests your app makes to another service.

technologies.name: PowerShell
technologies.explanation: Windows automation scripting. Useful for IT support, health checks, setup scripts, and admin tasks.

technologies.name: JavaScript
technologies.explanation: Browser-side code used to make pages interactive, respond to clicks, update the screen, and handle dynamic UI behavior.

technologies.name: Flask
technologies.explanation: A Python web framework. If this is detected, the project probably has pages, routes, forms, or a local web dashboard.

technologies.name: React
technologies.explanation: A JavaScript library for building reusable interface pieces called components. React is useful when a page has many changing parts.

technologies.name: SQLAlchemy
technologies.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.explanation: The project likely includes drills, lessons, quests, mastery tracking, or repeated practice.

capabilities.name: Project Tracking
capabilities.explanation: The project likely stores project progress, milestones, roadmap items, or build status.

capabilities.name: Health Monitoring
capabilities.explanation: The project likely checks whether something is working correctly, scans for issues, or reports status.

capabilities.name: Evidence Collection
capabilities.explanation: The project likely saves screenshots, attachments, proof, logs, or supporting evidence.

capabilities.name: Ticket System
capabilities.explanation: The project likely manages issues, incidents, root causes, resolutions, or support tickets.

capabilities.name: Task Management
capabilities.explanation: The project likely tracks tasks, statuses, checklists, or completions.

capabilities.name: Repair Planning
capabilities.explanation: The project likely suggests fixes, repairs files, creates backups, or plans recovery steps.

capabilities.name: Knowledge Base
capabilities.explanation: The project likely stores lessons, notes, memory, documentation, or reusable knowledge.

capabilities.name: Database Storage
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.name: Dashboard/UI
capabilities.explanation: The project has visible screens, forms, buttons, or dashboard pages. This means it is meant for a person to use, not just run silently.

capabilities.name: File Handling
capabilities.explanation: The project reads, writes, moves, scans, or extracts files. This is a major skill for tools like analyzers and automation apps.

capabilities.name: Journal/Reflection
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.name: Idea Capture
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.name: Automation/Scheduling
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.name: Notifications/Reminders
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

capabilities.name: Authentication/Login
capabilities.explanation: KDT OS detected this from keywords, files, and code patterns. Treat it as a clue, not a perfect conclusion.

risks.name: Broad exception handling
risks.explanation: The code catches errors too generally, usually with except Exception or bare except. This can hide real problems and make debugging harder. It does not mean the app is broken, but it means future repairs may be harder to trust.

risks.name: Hardcoded secret/key
risks.explanation: A password, API key, or secret may be written directly in the code. That is risky if the project is shared or uploaded to GitHub.

risks.name: Debug mode enabled
risks.explanation: Debug mode is useful while learning, but should not be used in a real public deployment because it can expose sensitive details.

recommendations.text: Track repeated practice attempts, mistakes, and mastery level so learning becomes measurable.
recommendations.explanation: This supports your learning style: repetition, mistakes, review, and mastery tracking.

recommendations.text: Preserve this as a reusable capability for KDT OS project inspection and self-health checks.
recommendations.explanation: This project contains a capability that can make KDT OS stronger later instead of being rebuilt from scratch.

learning_opportunities.why: This project uses Python. Practicing it separately will make the project easier to understand and improve.

suggested_artifact.title: Python Function Practice: Expense Totaler
suggested_artifact.goal: Practice Python functions, lists, loops, and simple output with an exact beginner build.
suggested_artifact.what_to_build: A command-line app that stores three expenses in a list and prints the total cost.
success_criteria: At least three expenses are added
steps: Create an empty list named expenses
Create add_expense(amount) that appends amount to expenses
Create calculate_total() that loops through expenses and returns the total
Call add_expense three times with different numbers
Create README.md with what you practiced
Zip the project folder and submit it

learning_opportunities.why: This project uses SQLite. Practicing it separately will make the project easier to understand and improve.

suggested_artifact.title: Workout Database CRUD App
suggested_artifact.goal: Practice saving, reading, updating, and deleting records in SQLite.
suggested_artifact.what_to_build: A small Python app that stores workouts with exercise name, sets, reps, and date.
steps: Create workouts table with id, exercise, sets, reps, date
Add a menu with choices 1 Add, 2 View, 3 Edit, 4 Delete, 5 Exit
proof: Include the .db file if one is created

suggested_artifact.title: API Weather Lookup App
suggested_artifact.goal: Practice fetch(), JSON data, button events, and DOM updates.
suggested_artifact.what_to_build: A small web app where the user types a city name, clicks a button, and sees weather data appear on the page.
requirements: Display temperature or sample weather result
Show an error message when needed
steps: Add result area with id result
In JS, select all three elements
Add click listener to the button
proof: Write in README what API or sample JSON you used

learning_opportunities.why: This project uses Health Monitoring. Practicing it separately will make the project easier to understand and improve.

suggested_artifact.title: Mini Health Checker
suggested_artifact.goal: Practice health monitoring by checking a tiny system and reporting whether it looks OK.
suggested_artifact.what_to_build: A Python command-line app that checks three fake or simple health values and prints a clear OK/WARNING report.
requirements: Create three checks: cpu_check, memory_check, and disk_check
Each check must return OK or WARNING
Create README.md explaining how to run it
success_criteria: At least three health checks exist
Output clearly shows OK or WARNING
The project can be uploaded as proof
steps: Create a new folder named mini_health_checker
Inside app.py, create a function named cpu_check() that returns OK
Create a main section that calls all three functions
Print each result on its own line
Print a final line that says Overall Health: Needs Attention if any check is WARNING
Create README.md with the exact command: python app.py
Zip the folder and submit it
